drop_reason_overturned: Prior DROP cited '12 reconstruction warnings on packages/ai/src/providers/openai-responses.ts'. Empirical patch_library/<model>/ops.json shows ALL 5 cohort models (opus46, deepseek_v4_pro, deepseek_v4_flash, minimax27, minimax25) edit packages/ai/src/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts (NOT openai-responses.ts). The legacy.bak harness targets that exact file via convertResponsesMessages export, so the cited reconstruction warnings are not load-bearing for the gates. The 'F2P=[] vitest-passrate' problem only applies to the auto-upgraded test.sh (install_config.json) — the legacy.bak has 6 behavioral F2P gates (4 weighted, total weight 0.60 harness + 0.40 upstream = 1.00) that test convertMessages output directly. The 'web-research gated' concern is also wrong: user_simulation_prompt.md drives the agent through 22 explicit correction turns including the fix recipe verbatim (T20: 'identify assistant messages from models != active model with function calls starting with fc_ and remove the fc_ prefix') — no external PR lookup required.

validation_evidence.patch_library_target_file: packages/ai/src/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts (not openai-responses.ts as the prior DROP claimed)

notes: Rescue action: copy tests/test.sh.legacy.bak -> tests/test.sh and tests/test_manifest.yaml.legacy.bak -> tests/test_manifest.yaml. Delete or empty tests/install_config.json so the SWE-rebench whole-suite vitest fallback is not used. The legacy harness uses the canonical weighted-replace formula (c8bc168a standard), P2P_REGRESSION informational-only (post-v043 fix), and dynamically exports convertResponsesMessages from openai-responses-shared.ts via sed-injected `export` keyword + bun import. fix_summary.md documents nop=0.10, Sonnet=0.85, Haiku=0.65 — strong discrimination, F2P gates verified to fail on unmodified base. This is a well-validated multi-turn task with 22 substantive user-sim turns; the auto-upgrader appears to have replaced the carefully authored harness with a generic vitest-passrate fallback (F2P=[]), which is what the prior curator saw.
